chapter_context.summary: Catalogues the severe bloodguilt, abominations, and social oppression of Jerusalem, comparing the house of Israel to dross gathered in the midst of a furnace to be melted down by the fire of the Lord's wrath.

outline.text: Detailed Catalog of Jerusalem's Bloodguilt, Lewdness, and Extortion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Detailed Catalog of Jerusalem's Bloodguilt, Lewdness, and Extortion (22:1-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Gathering of Israel as Dross in the Furnace for Melting
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Gathering of Israel as Dross in the Furnace for Melting (22:17-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Universal Corruption of Prophets, Priests, Princes, and Commoners
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Universal Corruption of Prophets, Priests, Princes, and Commoners (22:23-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 22, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
